result.summary: AfterShip’s discovered developer surface is its HTTP APIs: Tracking, Returns, and Commerce; all use the AfterShip API key, and Tracking also supports OAuth access tokens. No additional public MCP, GraphQL, or CLI surfaces were confirmed.

aftership_api_key.label: AfterShip API key
aftership_api_key.setup: Go to the AfterShip developer console at [API keys](https://organization.automizely.com/api-keys) and create or copy your API key for the product/API you’re using. The docs indicate this is the primary credential for the APIs. Send it in the required request header for the surface (see each API’s auth section).

aftership_oauth_token.label: AfterShip OAuth access token
aftership_oauth_token.setup: Use AfterShip’s OAuth flow from the docs for the API you’re integrating with, then use the resulting access token when calling the API. The OAuth pages are under the product docs (for example, Tracking OAuth at [Getting Started](https://www.aftership.com/docs/tracking/quickstart/authentication/oauth/getting-started)).
